title: Show HN: I built 63 AI bots to run my entire content business ($70/mo vs $4k/mo human team)
text:
Six months ago I was spending 22+ hours/week on content. Now: zero.

Built 63 Python bots + 17 Claude AI agents on GitHub Actions:

• Daily blog posts (Claude writes, bot publishes to our site)
• Weekly newsletters (Beehiiv API)
• 6 social platforms posted daily (Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram, Pinterest, Reddit, Medium)
• Cold outreach (200 personalized emails/day via Apollo)
• Brand monitoring every 4 hours (NewsAPI + Reddit + HN)
• Competitor intelligence weekly
• YouTube Shorts scripts + ElevenLabs voicing
• Digital product delivery (Gumroad webhook)
• SERP domination (Ahrefs API + content flooding)
• VPS passive income ($15-38/month from bandwidth sharing)

Stack: Python + Anthropic Claude API + GitHub Actions (free) + Netlify (free tier)
Cost: ~$187/month total. Equivalent human team: $4,000-8,000/month.

Each bot is a standalone Python file. The "orchestrator" agent (inspired by Alex Mercer, CEO of our fictional internal agency) writes a morning brief that all agents read before executing. Failures auto-retry. The whole thing is self-repairing.

Questions welcome: architecture, cost optimization, which Claude models work best for what tasks, how to avoid GitHub Actions rate limits, anything.
note: CHAIRMAN ACTION REQUIRED: 3 minutes. Copy title + text. Submit at URL above. Best time listed above.
